briefs.title: Proposed: Port Moody Council to Read Territorial Land Acknowledgement at April 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to open its April 21, 2026 meeting with a Territorial Land Acknowledgement — a formal statement recognizing that the city operates on the ancestral and unceded (never formally surrendered) homelands of eight Indigenous Peoples, including the Kwikwetlem, Tsleil-Waututh, Musqueam, and Squamish Nations. This is a standing agenda item and does not propose any new rules or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Standing Committee Agenda for April 21, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Council Standing Committee is set to meet on April 21, 2026, to consider several items that could affect local businesses and the community. Topics up for discussion include exterior upgrades to the civic complex, a five-year financial plan update, transportation improvements on St. Johns Street and Barnet Highway, a request for additional capital budget for road design, and confirming policies related to family-friendly housing units.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 21, 2026 meeting live at zoom or City Hall to stay informed on decisions affecting your area.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council Standing Committee meeting — April 21, 2026

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Approves April 21, 2026 Standing Committee Agenda
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 21, 2026 Council Standing Committee meeting. This is a routine procedural step — it simply confirms the list of topics the committee will discuss that day. No business decisions are made at this stage.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Public Input Procedures at Port Moody Meeting — April 21, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will hold a public input session at its upcoming meeting on April 21, 2026. Members of the public can speak for up to two minutes or submit written comments. Council asks that all input stay respectful and focus on issues rather than individuals, in line with the City's conduct policies.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 21 meeting or submit written input if you want your voice heard by Council.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council public input session — comment opportunity

briefs.title: Council to vote on adopting Port Moody Standing Committee minutes from March 31, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on formally adopting the minutes from its March 31, 2026 Council Standing Committee meeting. Those minutes capture several decisions up for ratification, including proposed Amenity Cost Charges (fees developers pay to fund community amenities), a new Farmers Market location at the Old Fire Hall site backed by $90,000 in city funding, and updates to pay parking rates.
briefs.action_required: If you are a developer or market vendor in Port Moody, watch for the ratification of these decisions — they may affect your costs or location.

briefs.title: Council to Reconvene: Port Moody Standing Committee to Continue Agenda Items
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Council Standing Committee is set to reconvene after its Governance and Legislation Committee meeting wraps up, so it can work through the rest of its agenda. No specific business items are detailed in this notice — it simply signals that the meeting will continue and more decisions may be considered.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Port Moody Council agenda to see if any upcoming items affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to vote on ratifying Port Moody committee decisions from April 21, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Council Standing Committee is set to vote on ratifying a package of decisions made by its sub-committees on April 21, 2026. Items up for approval include road and transportation upgrades, a request for extra capital budget funding, updates to the five-year financial plan, and resolutions related to family-friendly housing units.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 21, 2026 Port Moody Council meeting to track decisions that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council Standing Committee meeting — ratification vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Ratify: Port Moody Committee Recommendations from April 21, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on ratifying — that is, formally approving — all recommendations passed by four committees on April 21, 2026: City Initiatives and Planning, Finance, Strategic Priorities, and Governance and Legislation. The specific decisions made in those committees are not detailed here, so business owners should review the individual committee reports to understand what may be affected.
briefs.action_required: Review the April 21, 2026 Port Moody committee reports to see if any decisions affect your business.
